reasoning: The current ICE BofA US Corporate Index Effective Yield closed around 4.73% at the end of February 2026, slightly down from earlier in the month. Macroeconomic indicators and treasury markets suggest downward pressure on yields, with the 10-year Treasury rate falling from 4.31% in late January to ~4.04% in late February. The FRED statistical trend analysis estimates a slightly negative drift (-0.004% per day) yielding a combined model probability of 0.46 for the rate to exceed current levels. Given the moderate but persistent downward momentum in treasury yields and tight corporate spreads, the likelihood of an increase by March 8 is modestly below 50% (estimated at 42%). For the longer horizon to March 31, mean reversion and larger uncertainty pull the probability closer to an even coin flip (estimated at 46%), consistent with the requirement for uncertainty expanding over time.
